title: Implement persistent .agent/errors.jsonl logging for failed tool executions
summary: Add a persistent local error log for failed tool executions so tool errors are not only shown in the Zed agent panel but also appended to `.agent/errors.jsonl` with structured context including tool name, input, output/error, and timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Failed tool executions append one structured JSON line to `.agent/errors.jsonl`.
Each error record includes at least timestamp, tool name, tool input, and tool output/error details.
Error logging occurs at the tool execution boundary so both validation failures and runtime failures are captured.
Failure to append the error log does not suppress or replace the original tool error returned to the client.
Error logging never writes to `/.agent/errors.jsonl`; it must use the initialized and validated project root.

steps.description: Define the structured record schema for `.agent/errors.jsonl`, keeping it minimal but actionable.
Add repo-context support for resolving the error-log artifact path under the initialized project root.
Capture failed tool execution context in the JSON-RPC tool-call path and append it to `.agent/errors.jsonl`.
Ensure error logging is best-effort so logging failures do not mask the original client-facing error.

notes: Prefer JSONL append-only logging to keep the artifact easy to inspect and robust under repeated failures.
Do not over-capture unrelated request data; focus on tool name, tool input, output/error, and timestamp.
This ticket is about runtime error observability and should align with the workspace initialization/root safety model.
